title: Service Dependency Blast Radius Routing
description: An incident-and-change response workflow that coordinates mitigation, approvals, and execution sequencing under operational urgency. Focus case: service dependency blast radius routing.
assumptions: Engineers are allocated exclusively to a case while assigned; assignment prevents concurrent conflicting work.
Routing tokens and dependency reservations are unique per case and enforce mutual exclusion for sensitive operations.
Approvals can be represented as discrete, atomic predicates and are required before gated mitigation actions execute.
Mitigation actions are designed to be idempotent or reversible to limit unintended blast radius expansion.
risks: Incomplete or incorrect mapping of upstream/downstream dependencies can cause unanticipated service impact.
Race conditions if multiple cases attempt to reserve the same routing_token or dependency simultaneously.
Stale approvals or credentials may prevent timely execution, prolonging outage or increasing blast radius.
Overly coarse stakeholder grouping may delay approvals and slow mitigation under urgent conditions.
